reviews.author: Kim Chng
reviews.text: Back to true nature. Plenty of mosquitoes, leeches, tall trees and everything you want to find in a forest. Came here for birding and managed to snap 43 different species of birds within two hours. Was very disappointed with the last hide where there were so many leeches in the Hide that we had to abandon the place. There is only one cafe that serves delicious food and the staff are friendly.

reviews.author: Scott Miller
reviews.text: Had no clue that it was Vietnam's number one in UNESCO Park. Didn't realize that during the American War, agent Orange was sprayed all over the village thus destroying all the foilage around. But now the park has regrown and mother nature has done a a wonderful job. Highly recommend rent a bicycle to pedal around and check out all the different exhibits as you cruise about the national park. Staff extremely friendly and helpful good food choices and great beverages also a fun bun ride across the river to get to it.

reviews.author: Tony
reviews.text: Went on many excursions both for adventure and support. The hike to crokodile lake is nice, take time to wander in peace and Spot monkeys and other species during the path. We saw 3 different kind of apes. You need to hike the last 5km there and 5km back and to this spot you can either bicykle or get a ride with jeep (10km). Saw croks and they were close.. :) Animal rescue center + small museum is worth a visit. Good cause and specially to watch the gibbons eat at very close range was incredibly nice. Many other animals there too. Night safari was okay. The lady spotted the animals, but when people took photos she often pointed the flashlight straight into the animals edes. They obviously ran away after this. The route was a back n forth. The car broke down in the middle of the road but was soon fixed. Maybe the least nice trip but worth a go anyway. Suggest 1. Crok lake. 2. Rescue. 3. Night+ any other you wish to go on.

reviews.author: Thuyen Vu
reviews.text: First time visiting Cat Tien National Park and it was awesome. There are plenty of activities here such as bird watching, night safari, Bau Sau Lake visiting, biking... its great to be back and connect with the nature!

reviews.author: Phuong Hoai Nguyen
reviews.text: Having a nice walk in the park, entrance ticket was only 60.000 vnd. Beautiful trees and trails. Love it!

reviews.author: abram sandoval
reviews.text: This nation park is so much fun and lots to do. Today, we biked 18km and walked 10km to the crocodile lake. It was such a beautiful place. We also saw some amazing trees that were over 400 years old. We stopped at the local spa, and I had the best massage in my life, too. I fell in love with this area. There is also no tourist so it's chill.

reviews.author: Y. P. ([ Adv. ])
reviews.text: 🌿 Vietnam’s Wild Heart: My Unforgettable Jungle Journey in Cat Tien National Park 🌄 Why Cat Tien Stole My Heart - UNESCO Biosphere Reserve: One of Vietnam’s last lowland rainforests, spanning 70,000+ hectares of emerald wilderness . - Wildlife Galore: Gibbons, elephants, sun bears, and 350+ bird species. Spot critically endangered Siamese crocodiles at Bau Sau Lake! . - Conservation Hero: Rescue centers for bears and primates—your visit funds their survival . --- 🌟 Top Jungle Adventures 1. Gibbon Trek at 4:30 AM - Wake to a primal symphony: Hear yellow-cheeked gibbons’ haunting calls echo through the canopy as dawn breaks. We saw a family swinging just 20m away! - Tip: Only 4 spots/day—book MONTHS ahead. Worth every penny of 1,000,000 VND (~$40) . 2. Crocodile Lake (Bau Sau) - Cycle 9km through dripping jungle, hike 5km past 400-year-old trees, then glide by boat through a swamp dotted with crocs. Felt like Jurassic Park! . - Pro move: Sleep at the ranger station to hear the forest come alive at night . 3. Night Safari - Ride an open jeep with spotlights to see sambar deer, civets, and glowing-eyed owls. Pure magic! (200,000–300,000 VND) . 4. Ta Lai Ethnic Village - Kayak to a village of the Ma and Stieng people. Learned traditional weaving and ate lemongrass fish by a fire . 5. Giant Tung Tree - A 20m-tall, 400-year-old giant. Felt humbled standing beneath its roots . 🛶 Logistics: Navigate Like a Pro - Getting There: - From Ho Chi Minh City: 4hr bus (85,000 VND from Mien Dong Station) → Boat across Dong Nai River (60,000 VND) . - Drive yourself? Motorbike via QL20 highway—epic scenery! . - Best Time: Dec–May (dry season). Avoid Jun–Nov—leeches love the mud! . - Stay Inside the Park: - 🌿 Forest Floor Lodge: Fall asleep to frog choruses (~$65/night) . - 🏕️ Ta Lai Longhouse: Bamboo stilt house run by locals—social impact stay (~$20/person) . --- 🚨 Essential Tips - Pack: Leech socks, headlamp, binoculars, cash (no ATMs!) . - Eat: Yellow Bamboo Restaurant’s lemongrass tofu—fuel for adventures . - Ethics: Skip animal shows. Support ethical sanctuaries like the Bear Rescue Center (150,000 VND) . --- 💚 Final Thoughts Cat Tien isn’t a manicured “tourist experience.” It’s raw, muddy, and utterly alive. For those craving wild Vietnam beyond city chaos, this is your sacred grove. I left with bug bites, muddy boots, and a heart full of jungle magic ✨.

reviews.author: Susan Dobbs
reviews.text: Trucks bikes and trails. Lots of birding. Trail to crocodile lake with critically endangered crocodiles 🐊 it is 5 km each way

reviews.author: Lucas Lamy
reviews.text: We paid 60k for the entrance and 150k per bike (full day). Very reasonable price considering how large the park is, and we saw an uncountable number of monkeys (different species!). You need to be attentive during the bike ride, but as soon as branches move: stop and observe the treetops! The centuries-old trees are also magnificent. We recommend the place 100%!

reviews.author: Aud T
reviews.text: Had a great time there. The trip was nice, the food was good, and the staff was kind. If you visit it during the raining season, watch out for leeches and mosquitoes. They're no joke.

reviews.author: Thinh Nguyen Dinh Vu
reviews.text: Nice natural place for those love nature, relaxing, trekking, cycling with fresh environmont, river

reviews.author: Marc J.
reviews.text: Great place for nature lovers. Many places to go and you can rent a bycicle and go anywhere

reviews.author: Florent Henry
reviews.text: Book your tours directly with guides, don't go through hosts that take a substantial commission and hurt your experience there to feel like a walking wallet. If you rent bikes outside the park, you'll have to pay an extra 100.000 VND entrance fee for each crossing. I suspect the bike rental that is on the other side to benefit greatly form it as he becomes competitive even though the prices are expensive (a motorbike is usually 150.000 VND per day in the country and fits 2 persons - well, bikes are the same price per bike). Besides these practical informations, there are many animals to observe if your attentive and nature can be beautiful. Avoid weekends.

reviews.author: Phuc Vo
reviews.text: This is a place for nature lovers - Nam Cat Tien national park. Crocodile Lake is home to over 200 Siamese crocs and is one of the Cat Tien National Park highlights, it was designated a Ramsar site on August 4, 2005. I have been here recently with two friends for birding. Highly recommended.

reviews.author: Jérémie Lachance
reviews.text: WOW!!! This national park is a worthy detour for ANY NATURE LOVER! Enter the park early for a chance to see the apes! We were lucky enough to spot two gibbon groups and a few silver langur! The wildlife over there is so cool! The trails are easy enough to follow once you are in the park (i suggest downloading alltrails map app). The only downside is that you have to be willing to organise your transport there and back, which is not super easy but the Futa bus company at Nga Tu Ga helped us get there! Go for the love of gibbons!

reviews.author: sơn nguyễn
reviews.text: A lovely place for people who like staying close with nature. In this park, you can take a leisure walk between hundreds of yearolds trees, watch animal eating food at night. But i can't give this place a 5* because the room i stayed in was filled with moldy air, extremely unpleasant smell which affected my sleep. Foods were okay, not good, not bad.

reviews.author: ash w
reviews.text: so beautiful, end of bike trail there is a lovely swimming hole. it's about 30 minutes from crocodile lake

reviews.author: Dschanni
reviews.text: Prices of the park are quite high (European level). The gibbon tour (€35/person) is very recommendable even though expensive. Don't book the night safari! You're with 25 other people on a loud truck while the "guide" is pointing with laser in the faces of the animals - in our case f.e. an owl. It broke my heart. In addition small animals can't be seen when you not sitting in front next to the guide who is not informing about anything of the seen animals. Very disappointing! Seeing and smelling these contaminating trucks driving all day through this wonderful place is horrible. The crocodile lake is 30min bike (9.5 km) and one hour walking (5km). You can have a cold dring there and wait for the crocodiles. They will always show up :) The rental bike is a thing, too ... we came back and had not "our bikes". Somebody took them and left us their broken ones. It's a very special place, but it would be great the money would be used to take more care about it.

reviews.author: Tom Mossong
reviews.text: To get to Cát Tiên national park from Saigon by local bus: Took local bus 59 from near HCM airport to Nga Tu Ga Bus station 6,000 VND each 35 - 40 minute trip Spoke with ticket counter and was directed to a parked bus. Bus left 1hr later at 1300. Front window read "Da Lat" Paid 110,000 VND/person to conductor on bus once underway Bus was clean and air-conditioned. Got a little full with two people sitting on plastic chairs in the isle. Stopped for ~40 - 60 minutes for police inspection of some sort before leaving Saigon, and for a couple of minutes halfway for bathroom. Multiple brief stops to drop of packages or passengers. Left at 1300, arrived at 1830 - 5hr 30min total time. Route on Google maps says 3hr 30mins. Arrived in Cát Tiên village, about 10 mins walk from national park entrance ferry. Overall very smooth and pleasant process other than taking a bit longer than expected. Bus back was same deal, left at 1000 from beside national park ferry ticket counter. Asked host at accommodation re timing. 110k each. Did a 2hr boat trip in the morning with our accommodation host (Forest side eco lodge) and saw heaps of monkeys and kingfishers. National Park was nice but minimal wildlife seen. In the park we rented very tired but functional mountain bike for 150k for 8hrs. No helmets. Park is mainly one big road with two arms at a Y intersection. About 30km of cycling total. Didn't do crocodile lake due to timing and cost (250k pp) but heard good things about it afterwards. Has a ~10km return walk. Really the only longer walk to be found in the park. All other off shoots were 100 to 200m walks to trees or rapids. Could have done it all in one day if we went a little earlier. Bring water and snacks. Both available in the park but more expensive.

reviews.author: Warbr33d
reviews.text: My review is only aimed at the management. The nature is excellent with many species. But the park is let down by its management. The park earns a lot through its various offerings, yet it cannot even fix the dilapidated road leading to the Crocodile Lake. The various watchtowers are all in need of maintenance; one even has an entire wooden step missing. Very dangerous! Also the park unnecessarily removed vegetation around Crocodile Lake, which results in less birds to see. I also dislike the exorbitant 250k fee to Crocodile Lake although I'm torn on this issue since it might be necessary to restrict the flow of people due to the narrow path there. The trucks which shatter the tranquility of the forest (very annoying) also need to be replaced; nobody wants to breathe their toxic fumes while waking through the forest. That's the very thing we're trying to avoid by coming here. For the premier wilderness area in South Vietnam, there is much work needed to be done.

reviews.author: Thach Nguyen
reviews.text: Nam Cat Tien National Park, a natural park located in Dong Nai Province. Better choice for a weekend cycling trip with your friends and family. This park will make you feel better and more comfortable with the fresh air.

reviews.author: Petra Fischer
reviews.text: If you want to see a beautiful jungle to explore: here you go. It’s a 3,5 h trip from Saigon to Cat Tien and worth it. We were amazed by all our experiences at this wonderful place. It’s all about nature and I have not seen anything like this before. Easy excess by a ferry boat and easy rent a bike to visit the most popular places. If you like forest it’s your place to go. We hiked the first day on the dirt road to the rapids and the so called botanical garden with lots of botanical names of the trees. Everything is easy accessible and well guided by signs. We took incredible videos of the scenery. A place like garden Eden. True beautiful Vietnam.

reviews.author: Kate H
reviews.text: Very informative tour about the nature. Each tree has its owns stories to tell and with the right tour guide, you can learn a lot of knowledge that surprises you. Be cautious not going alone in the jungle as you will get lost. Lots of spiritual stories in the woods that even the locals not dare to stay.

reviews.author: Ivan Liljegren
reviews.text: The Cat Tien National Park is very well organized and maintained. There are botanical signs all around to help you identify trees and plants. We booked two tours through our lodge, and were blessed to have the exceptional guide Bè on both. The first day she took out on the Crocodile Lake hike where she showed us monkeys, lizards, spiders, birds as well as the crocodiles. She knows all the details about the park and its animals. The second morning before dawn she brought us on the gibbon tour, where we were lucky to see both gibbons and langurs having breakfast in the treetops. We are really thankful for these exceptional tours and for Bè’s expert guidance which gave us fond memories for life. We also rented bikes for a short trek to the rapids. They are beautiful but not as exceptional as the jungle tours.

reviews.author: Joshua Calhoun
reviews.text: A very special and unique place on earth. Many many days are truly required to really appreciate and experience all that this national park has to offer. Take your time and enjoy its tranquility.

reviews.author: Jeff Chane-mouye
reviews.text: Le Gibbons wild trek was amazing. We were able to see them. The crocodile lake was the place of the langurs

reviews.author: Thuan Cao
reviews.text: Lucky to have seen Silver Langurs in their natural habitats.

reviews.author: Vera Williams
reviews.text: I only hung out here for half a day. It was ok. The path to walk or bike around the area is awful - not comfy at all to ride with stone pavement cracked, bumpy and with holes. Maybe it would be better if you were here for longer but it doesn’t strike me as a place to go out of your way for. 30K for 1hr bike rental.

reviews.author: Nguyễn Công Bách
reviews.text: Peaceful place, cozy atmosphere, space and time just stop for a while when you tracking in to the forest, recommended for who is looking for quiet place.

reviews.author: Trang Thu Nguyen
reviews.text: pretty place but should prepare for mosquitoes
